fact: March 9 is exactly 68 days into the year, which means there are only 297 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 9 falls smack in the middle of when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
Why do cats seem extra crazy around this time? March 9 sits right in the peak of 'kitten season' when outdoor cats start having babies because of longer daylight hours!
The sun rises about 6 minutes earlier on March 9 than it did just one week before - that's like gaining a whole cartoon episode of extra morning light!
March 9 is a Pisces day, which means people born today are supposedly dreamy fish-people who love to swim in their own imagination!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 9 is when many spiders start getting ready for winter by spinning thicker, stronger webs!
The spring equinox is only about 11 days away from March 9, making this date part of the 'almost spring but not quite' zone where nature gets really confused!
March 9 falls during the time when earthworms start wiggling up to the surface after winter, making sidewalks extra squishy and gross after rain!
In cold places like Canada, March 9 is prime time for 'sugar snow' - the perfect sticky snow that's amazing for building snowmen but terrible for snowball fights!
The moon phase around March 9 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called a 'Worm Moon' because that's when worms start popping up everywhere!
March 9 sits right in the middle of when many trees start budding, which means the air gets filled with invisible pollen that makes people sneeze like crazy!
In many places, March 9 marks the time when hibernating animals like bears start stirring in their dens, getting ready to wake up super hungry and grumpy!
